title: monardella antonina subsp. benitensis
intro: Monardella antonina subsp. benitensis, also known as the Ben Lomond Mountain Monardella, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae, native to the southwestern United States. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties due to its high content of volatile oils and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Monardella antonina subsp. benitensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and provide antioxidant protection. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of this plant can be dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
